text:
Bella Hadidhas spoken out after her campaign withAdidaswas pulled and labelled an ‘egregious error’.

The model, 27, said she was ‘shocked and upset’ over the ‘lack of sensitivity’ in planning the campaign.

In the re-launch of the SL72 trainer – which was originally sold in 1972 – Bella could be seen holding a floral bouquet and posing with the shoes.

It’s been reported that the trainer was launched that summer to coincide with the MunichOlympics, where amassacre of 11 Israeli athletesoccurred in a terrorist attack.

The Palestinian group Black September infiltrated the Olympic Village, leaving two members of the Israeli Olympic team murdered and nine kidnapped.

The nine athletes who were taken hostage were also killed after a failed rescue attempt.

The release of the new Adidas campaign in 2024 sparked backlash after the reference to the 1972 Munich Olympics, as well as the fact that the firm chose to feature Hadid.

Hadid has regularly spoken out in support of Palestine as the war between Israel and Hamas continues in Gaza.

Her father is Palestinian and she has been a vocal critic of Israel even before the conflict escalated in October.

In a statement sent toNBC News, Adidas said that it was ‘revising the remainder of the campaign’.

‘We are conscious that connections have been made to tragic historical events – though these are completely unintentional – and we apologize for any upset or distress caused,’ the statement read.

The State of Israel had criticised the decision to feature Hadid in the campaign in a post written on X, while Combat Antisemitism Movement CEO Sacha Roytman called the collaboration ‘sick’.

The American Jewish Committee also wrote on the social media platform: ‘For Adidas to pick a vocal anti-Israel model to recall this dark Olympics is either a massive oversight or intentionally inflammatory. Neither is acceptable. We call on Adidas to address this egregious error.’

After initial reports she was looking into taking legal action against Adidas, Hadid made a statement on Instagram.

She said she was ‘disappointed in the lack of sensitivity that went into this campaign’.

The model continued: ‘I would never knowingly engage with any art or work that is linked to a horrific tragedy of any kind. In advance of the campaign release, I had no knowledge of the historical connection to the atrocious events in 1972.

‘I am shocked, I am upset, and I am disappointed in the lack of sensitivity that went into this campaign. Had I been made aware, from the bottom of my heart, I would never have participated.

‘My team should have known, Adidas should have known and I should have done more research so that I too would have known and understood, and spoken up.’

According toTMZ, she has hired a legal team after the company demonstrated a ‘lack of public accountability’ for the campaign.

The publication claims that Hadid is still under contract with Adidas, despite the campaign being revised following outrage.

Metro.co.uk has contacted representatives of Bella Hadid and Adidas for comment.

title: Bella Hadid 'shocked and upset' to learn truth behind Adidas campaign | Metro News
description: An Adidas Olympics campaign that featured model Bella Hadid has been pulled after being labelled ‘sick’ and an ‘egregious error’.
